title: Destructuring with Default Values
explanation: a=1 (exists). b=20 (value is undefined → default applies). c=30 (missing → default applies). Defaults only kick in for undefined.
mentalModel: Destructuring defaults apply ONLY when value is undefined (missing or explicitly undefined)

title: Spread Merge — Order Determines Winner
explanation: Later properties overwrite earlier ones. In config1, userPrefs is last → theme='light'. In config2, defaults is last → theme='dark'.
mentalModel: Spread order = priority. Last spread wins for duplicate keys

title: Tagged Template Literals
explanation: Tagged templates: first arg = array of string parts (split by expressions). Rest args = interpolated values. So one=['', ' is ', ' years old'], two='Lydia', three=21.
mentalModel: Tagged templates: first arg = string parts array, rest args = interpolated values in order

title: Rest Parameter — Collects Remaining
explanation: x gets first element (1). ...y collects ALL remaining into an array [2, 3, 4]. Rest must be last.
mentalModel: ...rest = 'collect everything else into an array'. Must be the LAST parameter

title: Spread on String — Character Array
code: console.log([...'Lydia']);
explanation: Strings are iterables. Spreading a string gives an array of individual characters.
mentalModel: Strings are iterables. Spreading a string = array of characters

title: Destructuring Rename — Original Undefined
explanation: { firstName: myName } assigns value to myName, not firstName. firstName variable is never created. typeof returns 'undefined'.
mentalModel: { key: alias } = creates alias, NOT the key. Key is a lookup path, not a variable

title: Default Parameters — Evaluated at Call Time
explanation: First two: default { ...value } creates fresh copy each time → 10*2=20. Third: passes value directly → 10*2=20, mutates value.number to 20. Fourth: passes mutated value → 20*2=40.
mentalModel: Default params create new values each call. Passing an object directly shares the reference

title: Map vs Object — Function as Key
explanation: Map allows any value as key, including functions. myMap.get(myFunc) works because it's the SAME reference. But () => 'greeting' creates a NEW function — different reference → undefined.
mentalModel: Map keys use reference identity. Two identical-looking functions are different objects in memory

title: Set — Unique Values Only
code:
const set = new Set([1, 1, 2, 3, 4]);
console.log(set);
explanation: Set stores only unique values. Duplicate 1 is removed. The resulting set has 4 values: {1, 2, 3, 4}.
mentalModel: Set = no duplicates. Pass an array to constructor to de-duplicate

title: Symbol Uniqueness — Same Description, Different Value
code: console.log(Symbol('foo') === Symbol('foo'));
explanation: Every Symbol is entirely unique. The description ('foo') is just a label — it doesn't affect the identity. Two Symbols with the same description are still different values.
mentalModel: Symbol('x') !== Symbol('x'). Descriptions are labels, not identifiers

title: Symbol Not Enumerable — Hidden from Object.keys
explanation: Symbol-keyed properties are NOT enumerable. Object.keys only returns enumerable string keys → empty array. But console.log shows all properties including Symbols.
mentalModel: Symbols are invisible to Object.keys/for-in. Use Object.getOwnPropertySymbols to access them

title: for-in vs for-of — Keys vs Values
explanation: for-in iterates over ENUMERABLE KEYS (indexes for arrays). for-of iterates over ITERABLE VALUES (the actual elements).
mentalModel: for-in = keys (enumerable properties). for-of = values (iterable elements)

title: import is Pre-parsed — Runs Before Module Code
explanation: ES module imports are pre-parsed and run FIRST, regardless of where the import statement appears in the file. sum.js executes before any code in index.js.
mentalModel: import = pre-parsed. Imported modules ALWAYS run before the importing module

title: import * as — Default and Named Exports
explanation: import * as data imports ALL exports as properties of a namespace object. The default export is available as data.default, named exports as data.name.
mentalModel: import * creates a namespace object. Default export = .default property. Named = their property names

title: Imported Modules are Read-Only
output: TypeError (or Error): Assignment to constant variable / read-only
explanation: Imported bindings are read-only — you cannot modify them from the importing module. Only the exporting module can change its own values.
mentalModel: Imports are live read-only bindings. You can observe changes but can't mutate from outside

title: Object.entries — Iterate Keys and Values
explanation: Object.entries returns an array of [key, value] pairs. for-of iterates over them, and we destructure each pair into x (key) and y (value).
mentalModel: Object.entries = [[key, value], ...]. Combine with for-of + destructuring for clean iteration

title: Generator yield vs yield* — Array vs Elements
explanation: yield returns the whole value as-is (the array). yield* delegates to another iterable, yielding each element individually. First next() of yield* returns 'a'.
mentalModel: yield = return whole value. yield* = delegate and iterate inner iterable one-by-one

title: Optional Chaining (?.) — Safe Property Access
explanation: ?. short-circuits if the left side is null/undefined — returns undefined instead of throwing. person.pet exists → name = 'Mara'. pet.family is undefined → returns undefined. getFullName exists and is invocable.
mentalModel: ?. = if it exists, access it. If null/undefined, return undefined instead of crashing

title: String.raw — Escape Characters Ignored
code: console.log(String.raw`Hello\nworld`);
explanation: String.raw is a built-in tag function that returns the raw string — escape sequences like \n are NOT processed. They appear as literal backslash + n.
mentalModel: String.raw = no escape processing. \n stays as literal \n, not a newline

title: JSON.stringify — Replacer Array
explanation: The second argument to JSON.stringify is a replacer. When it's an array, only properties listed in the array are included in the JSON output. 'username' is excluded.
mentalModel: JSON.stringify replacer array = whitelist of properties to include

title: Array Methods — push Returns Length
explanation: .push() returns the NEW LENGTH of the array, not the array itself. A common mistake is to chain methods on the return of push.
mentalModel: .push() returns the new length (number), NOT the modified array

title: map Without Return — Array of undefined
explanation: The typeof check is true for all elements, so the function returns undefined (bare return). The map function creates a new array of the returned values — all undefined.
mentalModel: map() ALWAYS returns an array of the same length. Missing returns become undefined

title: defineProperty — Not Enumerable by Default
explanation: Properties added via defineProperty are NOT enumerable by default. Object.keys only returns enumerable properties. console.log shows all properties including non-enumerable ones.
mentalModel: defineProperty defaults: enumerable=false, writable=false, configurable=false. Invisible to Object.keys

title: Setter Returns undefined
explanation: The getter returns a formatted string. Note: calling a setter (bird.sound = 'x') always returns undefined — setters don't hold values, they modify properties.
mentalModel: Getter returns a computed value. Setter's purpose is to modify — it always returns undefined

title: Array Destructuring — Only First Value
code:
const [y] = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
console.log(y);
explanation: [y] destructures only the first element. The remaining 2, 3, 4, 5 are ignored. To capture them, use [...rest].
mentalModel: Destructuring extracts values by position. Unmatched positions are ignored

title: padStart — Pad to Target Length
explanation: padStart(13) adds 1 space (12 chars + 1 = 13). padStart(2) adds nothing because the string is already longer than 2 characters. It never truncates.
mentalModel: padStart pads to target length. If string is already longer than target, nothing happens

title: const Object — Mutation Allowed, Reassignment Not
explanation: const prevents reassignment (emojis = [...] would fail). But it does NOT prevent mutation — .push(), .splice(), property changes are all allowed.
mentalModel: const = can't reassign the variable. CAN mutate the value (if it's an object/array)

title: Default Params — Use Previous Params
explanation: num2's default is num1. We only pass 10 for num1, so num2 defaults to num1 = 10. 10 + 10 = 20. Defaults can reference earlier params, but not later ones.
mentalModel: Default parameters can reference PREVIOUS parameters (left-to-right). Not later ones

title: Proxy — Custom Get and Set Behavior
explanation: Proxy traps intercept operations. Setting person.name triggers the set trap → logs 'Set!'. Accessing person.name triggers the get trap → logs 'Get!' and returns 'Lydia'.
mentalModel: Proxy = middleware for objects. Intercept get/set/delete/has operations with custom logic

title: Object.seal vs Object.freeze
explanation: Object.seal: can MODIFY existing properties but can't ADD or DELETE properties. person.name changes to 'Sarah'. person.age is a new property → silently fails. Nested objects are NOT sealed → address.street changes.
mentalModel: seal: can modify, can't add/delete. freeze: can't modify/add/delete. Both are shallow

title: Private Class Fields (#) — SyntaxError
explanation: Private fields (#) cannot be accessed outside the class. counter.#number throws SyntaxError. You must use the public method getNum() to access it.
mentalModel: # = truly private. Access ONLY inside the class body. Not even subclasses can see it

title: Array slice vs splice vs unshift
explanation: slice does NOT modify original (returns sliced copy). splice DOES modify (removes '🍌'). unshift DOES modify (adds '🍇' to start). Result: ['🍇', '🍊', '🍎'].
mentalModel: slice = non-destructive (returns new). splice/unshift = destructive (modify original)

title: Intl.NumberFormat — Locale Formatting
explanation: Intl.NumberFormat formats numbers based on locale and options. unit style formats as speed. currency style adds dollar sign and decimal places.
mentalModel: Intl.NumberFormat = locale-aware number formatting. Supports currency, percentage, units, etc.

title: Object.fromEntries — Array to Object
explanation: Object.fromEntries takes an array of [key, value] pairs and creates an object. map transforms keys into pairs. Inverse of Object.entries.
mentalModel: Object.fromEntries = [[key, value], ...] → { key: value }. Inverse of Object.entries
